finiteActiveRangeIdentityStatus.label: finite active range identity
finiteActiveRangeIdentityStatus.reason: Finite active blocks satisfy E_active=C_N R_N to roundoff.

sampledGreenDensityStatus.label: sampled Green density construction
sampledGreenDensityStatus.reason: Rows of C_N define sampled kernels K_N(a_i)=C_{N,i}/w_i.

traceGridDensityStabilityStatus.label: trace-grid density stability
traceGridDensityStabilityStatus.reason: Weighted density norms remain stable under trace refinement; this is finite evidence for an L2 trace-dual kernel.

lagrangeAdjointIdentityStatus.label: Lagrange adjoint identity
lagrangeAdjointIdentityStatus.reason: The symbolic identity D_a B_P[h,f]=h Lambda_a(f)-f P^*h is verified to roundoff for source test rows.

sampledKernelAdjointIbpStatus.label: sampled-kernel adjoint IBP diagnostic
sampledKernelAdjointIbpStatus.reason: Differentiating the coarse pseudoinverse kernel K_N does not solve the adjoint boundary problem; this diagnostic is expected to stay open until K_u is constructed by the BVP itself.

continuumAdjointGreenProblemStatus.label: continuum adjoint Green problem
continuumAdjointGreenProblemStatus.reason: Still need a self-contained construction of K_u solving the adjoint boundary problem P^*K_u=eta_u with the endpoint concomitant matching the active source row.

adjointBoundaryDiagnostic.interpretation: Finite-difference P^*K_N and endpoint concomitant diagnostic. Large IBP defects indicate the need for an analytic adjoint Green construction rather than differentiating coarse sampled kernel coefficients.

exactActiveRangeOnRegularizedKernel.label: exact active range on regularized kernels
exactActiveRangeOnRegularizedKernel.reason: Polynomial densities solve the two active moment equations.

regularizedAdjointIbpSmall.label: regularized adjoint IBP diagnostic
regularizedAdjointIbpSmall.reason: Tests whether the smooth finite kernels behave like solutions of P^*K=eta with matched endpoint concomitant.

continuumBvpClosed.label: continuum adjoint BVP compactness
continuumBvpClosed.reason: Still requires a uniform norm bound and convergence of the regularized kernels in the trace-dual topology.

activeEndpointMapFullRowRank.label: active endpoint map full row rank
activeEndpointMapFullRowRank.reason: Sufficient but not necessary; the diagnostic may still close if the actual endpoint right-hand sides lie in Range(M).

actualEndpointRhsInRange.label: actual endpoint RHS in Range(M)
actualEndpointRhsInRange.reason: For the actual source rows, the endpoint vectors b(d) are in the finite endpoint-map range.

activeEndpointKilled.label: active endpoint concomitant killed
activeEndpointKilled.reason: Minimum-norm z solves Mz=-b(d) on the active two-dimensional source space.

uniformTraceDualBoundClosed.label: uniform trace-dual bound
uniformTraceDualBoundClosed.reason: Still needs a rigorous continuum bound for the exact fundamental matrix and M^+ over the source window.

formalConstruction: Finite: E_active=C_N R_N on the source-active block.
Quadrature interpretation: C_{N,i}=w_i K_N(a_i), so K_N(a_i)=C_{N,i}/w_i.
Continuum target: P_delta E_u(f)=P_delta int_I K_u(a)Lambda_a(f) da.
Lagrange identity converts this into the adjoint Green boundary problem P^*K_u=eta_u plus endpoint concomitant terms.
The actual source rows are distributional at s0; their interior contribution is encoded by exact jumps of K,K',...,K^(7).
After the jumps are fixed, the endpoint concomitant is affine in z; the finite ODE diagnostic solves the actual source endpoint range condition.
The coarse sampled K_N should not be differentiated as the BVP solution; the adjoint-boundary diagnostic records that failure mode.
A polynomial minimum-norm regularized K_N improves the IBP defect but still does not close the BVP.
remainingAnalyticGap: Solve the endpoint concomitant part of the adjoint Green boundary problem after the now-derived interior source jump law, then prove compactness/convergence of the regularized kernels in the trace-dual norm.
interpretation: The finite active range identity and trace-grid density stability support the continuum trace-to-source representation.  The actual continuum adjoint Green problem remains the next analytic proof.
